equipment.name: Carcano M91/38
equipment.notes: The standard Italian infantry rifle. The calibre was changed from 6.5mm to 7.35mm in 1938 for better stopping power, but wartime shortages meant many soldiers continued using older 6.5mm weapons.

equipment.name: Beretta MAB 38
equipment.notes: Considered one of the finest submachine guns of the war. Adopted by Italian, German, and Romanian forces. Its double-trigger design (one for semi-auto, one for full-auto) was innovative.

equipment.name: Breda M30
equipment.notes: Italy's standard squad automatic weapon. Criticised for its unusual 20-round feed strip (which had to be oiled before loading) and tendency to jam. Replaced gradually by the Breda M37.

equipment.name: Breda M37
equipment.notes: Italy's standard medium machine gun from 1937. More reliable than the M30. Used a tray feed system; fired cartridges were reinserted into the feed tray (a distinctive feature).
